title: metrosideros scariosa
intro: Metrosideros scariosa, also known as the New Zealand honeysuckle or Kawakawa, is a species of flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's extracts have been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and treating skin conditions and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Metrosideros scariosa are often infused or used in teas, tinctures, and creams to harness its medicinal properties.
